123:     output += index + 1 < bytes.length ? alphabet[(value >> 6) & 63] ?? '' : '=';

- `packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deployment-runner.ts:1724` requeues a deployment (`status='queued'`) on retryable errors even after a provider deployment was already created at `packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deployment-runner.ts:1109`. On the next attempt, execution always calls `provider.createDeployment(...)` again instead of resuming `providerDeploymentId`, so transient polling/cancel API failures can create duplicate remote deployments for the same Nimbus deployment. This is an idempotency break in the cancel/retry path.
- In inline mode (no queue), cancel reconciliation can dead-end: `handleCreateWorkspaceDeployment` only triggers a one-shot `waitUntil(runWorkspaceDeploymentInlineWithRetries(...))` at `packages/worker/src/api/workspace-deployments.ts:582`. If runner hits `"cancel requested while running"` it loops briefly then returns at `packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deployment-runner.ts:1813`, leaving the deployment `running` with `cancel_requested_at` set and no guaranteed future driver to reach terminal state. That is a terminal-state correctness blocker for cancel/retry without queue redelivery.

I did not find an additional blocker in the precheck lease ownership logic itself; the lease compare-and-swap guards look sound for ownership handoff.
